CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-18-2008 Set For Trial On Jul-21-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Jul-02-08 At 08:30 A.M. In Dept 306

MOTION TO STRIKE
Off Calendar Nov-02-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS LEV YUROVSKY, TATYANA YUROVOSKY, ANDREY YUROVSKY AND TAMARA YUROBSKY'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (CCP SECTION 425.16).
APPEARANCES: Raquel Fox, Esq. of Tenderloin Housing Clinic 771-9850 x115 pltf & opposing party; James Brandan Kraus, Esq. of Zacks Utrecht & Leadbetter 956-8100 for defts & moving parties.
RULING: Argued and the Court "modified" its tentative ruling and adopted as follows: denied. The decision in Birkner v. Lam, 2007 WL 2730260, provides the framework for the Court's decision. Defendants' motion is based on the letter of August 7, 2006 and the owner move-in notice. Under Birkner, those documents reflect protected petitioning activity. However, plaintiff's complaint is based on more than these two documents as reflected in paragraphs 10 and 11. Further, at most only the 1st, 3rd and 8th causes of action could be said to rely upon protected conduct; however, a reading of the complaint demonstrates such conduct is only a part of the defendant's conduct about which plaintiff complains. As a consequence, the Court does not find that plaintiff's complaint is based upon protected activity. Assuming, however, that it is, plaintiff has presented prima facie facts that plaintiff is likely to prevail on the causes of action alleged. Plaintiff's declaration describes supporting a claim of retaliation, harassment and habitability. The fact that defendants did not follow through on the owner move-in "IS EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM." Although the litigation privilege may protect some of defendants' conduct, it is not a bar to the vast majority of facts upon which plaintiff relies. Plaintiff to prepare proposed order. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
